comments: a) Complete show (tape flip during FNC).
b) The tape cuts in during Pure Prairie League and cuts out during Jimi Hendrix. (§)

sessions.details: session for Andy Peebles. Recorded: 1979-06-07. First TX: 1979-06-18. No known commercial release.

track_listing.details: LP-The Power Of Rock And Roll; CBS

track_listing.track: 'Hello Hello' (LP-If I Could Do It All Over Again, I'd Do It All Over You)' (Decca)
'Don't Keep Me Hanging (LP-Something In The Night)' (Casablanca)

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Oh hello there, this is TV on the Radio here, Thomas the Vance, and welcome once again to The Friday Rock Show.
Right, tonight we repeat the first ever radio session that was laid down by Def Leppard way back in 1979.
Plus, the debut session from an excellent middlelands group who have a really good album out. They happen to be called Demon.
Plus some classics by Utopia, Caravan, and Jimi Hendrix, among others, and some new stuff from Blue Oyster Cult, Frank Marino, Genesis, Steve Hackett, Bernie Marsden, and Pure Prairie League.
Not forgetting the Friday Night Connection, the answer to last week's and a new one.
But tonight, we start with this.
And of course, it's Blue Oyster Cult. It's a single that's just been lifted from their brand new album, which I'm happy to say has gone in this week at a chance at number 29.
The single has the title Burning For You.
Before that, a couple of tracks by Rush,
Temples of Siddhinks and Overture,
for loads of people around the country,
including Ali Fraser,
or is that just Al Fraser from Norwich,
and his girlfriend Julie, who lives in Folkestone,
all the ex-duke of York's fifth-form headbangers
who used to inhabit Dover,
especially Bob and Jonathan H. and Ian
of the Green Lightbulb fame,
Jazz Kev, Russ, who's up there in Scotland somewhere,
Philton tracks from Dover,
not forgetting the infamous Alisson in Bournemouth,
at the moment,
and Chris Bowden as well
and Simon, Simon Fraser that is
the long-suffering brother
The Rush Track is also for Gabe and for Tom
and Marion and Ron and Ian and Gavin
and all the Rush lovers
from Ray Purvis of Denny in Stirlingshire
and for everybody who's associated
with the Littleborough Headbangers
Lee, Mark, Stephen and Krypton
from Simon Russell
Time for just one more
This is from Pam and Carl
the loving wife and son of a gentleman
who had a birthday on the 19th of July
That was Derek
Derek, hope you had a good one
Here is a good one, the first of four by Demon.
It's come out on CBS, which really is scintillating in its guitar playing.
The album's called The Power of Rock and Roll.
That track has the title Young Man.
And before that, the first of four from the first ever radio session by Demon.
The track was called Night of the Demon.
Got a card here from Joe, who's in Oxford, I think, at the moment,
says, could I please dedicate a record to a girl from Birmingham called Michelle,
who met a fellow by the name of Joe from Oxford on the Isle of Wight
while they were both on holiday.
Michelle, he says, I've got to tell you that he's going to be at Castle Donington.
Also, Joe wants me to say hello to Dave Lawrence and Nick Star-I-Yates, I think it is,
and everybody at the Hare and Hounds.
Nellie was 24 on the 31st of July.
All the bikers at the Hopleaf MCC are going to be sitting around their tranny
in Long Ray in the Lake District at the moment, having a good holiday.
I hope they come from Ambleside in Cumbria.
That's General and Andy and Chris and Carol and Donut and Winnie and Skelly,
not forgetting baby Al.
Here's the magic of a band called Utopia.
They sounded way back in 1979 when they recorded their first ever radio session.
That was Def Leppard, a thing that came out as a single called Wasted.
From Steve and Bamba, for Sue and Jalbel and Mary and Nell.
And also for Michael Jackson and Stephen Paul and David Washington.
They all come from Huddersfield, but I was having a drink with them in Malta a couple of weeks ago.
It's a Chisk, actually, which is the name of one of the local beers.
I'd also like to say hello to Jackie and Bob and Kev, who are twins,
and Sandra and Graham and Andy and Craig,
and both Martins and John and Kim and Kathy and Bob and Bernard and Kev and Jones.
But particularly to a lady by the name of Denise.
It is at 59 Alexandra Road, Canton in Cardiff.
She's 16 today. Sweet 16. Happy birthday, James.
Before that, something from a 1976 album called Ra.
The brilliance of Todd Rundgren and a band called Utopia
Overture and communion with the sun
And as for a very special lady whose name I don't have
But who is a very good friend of Colin Smith
It's 25 to 11, here's the new single by Genesis
A nice few singles there, that one was by Steve Hackett
That's out on Charisma, it's called Hope I Don't Wake in the Morning
You've heard the new single by Genesis, also on Charisma
It's title is Abba Cow
Steve Hackett track was specially for Martin Adelton
who lives at 82 Kirkston Drive, Loughborough in Leicestershire.
Ian Stewart, who's in Brighton in Sussex,
wanted me to play a Hackett track for George and Biffo and Tim and Adam
and all Genesis Stroke Hackett fans all over the place.
Nice card here from Marcia Hindle.
Marcia wanted me to play a Saxon single, but we've already done that,
so I hope you enjoyed the last two we played.
For Gary Hindle of Two, Night's Way, Great Dunmo in Essex.
With lots of love for his 60th birthday today from all the family.
Hope they give you a flitch.
I'll give you demon.
The session tracks there, that of course was Def Leppard from 1979
and Sorrow is a Woman, would you call that a sexist remark?
And before that, Demon from a really good session
that was laid down the other day with Tony Wilson
in the Maida Vale studios of the BBC.
The track was called One Hell of a Night, One Hell of a Session.
Eight minutes now to eleven o'clock.
The music in the background hails the advent of the Friday Night Connection yet again.
right the tracks we played for you last week were the shoot you in the back from ace of spades by
motorhead on bronze records cold as ice by foreigner from the album foreigner and stone
free by jimmy hendrix most everybody got it right you were really on form last week stone free stone
age cold as ice ice age bronze records the murderhead track bronze age not everybody can win
know. I'll tell you he has, though. Robert Stemmer, 35 Davis Avenue, Newton-Lewillows,
Merseyside. Good on you, Bob. Get yourself a £10 record token. Wish we could put that
up one day, but we haven't got the money. You know how it is in this economy of ours.
Though it still, I fear, remains a £10 record token. Something for nothing, and it's something
too for fun. Robert has put together a Friday night connection. Three bits of music that
definitely have a connection contained they're in start listening now last time like today
imagination of robert stemmer five davis avenue newton lorillas and merseyside how to get your
imagination running if you think you know what the connection is there is a definite connection
in those three pieces of music jot it down on a postcard letter any form of communication you
like and send it to the friday night connection the friday rock show bbc radio one broadcasting
Springhouse, London, W1A4WW.
Don't forget to put together your own Friday night connection as well, please.
And the person who wins gets a £10 record token.
Good luck.
New studio of the BBC, which really is impeccable.
Combination like, and of course, a darn good engineer as well.
That was Demon, that part of our track, part of our session by then.
That was called Father of Time.
Before that, a new single by Bernie Marsden.
That's just out on Parlophone, and it's called Look At Me Now.
At the moment, in the same studio where Demon recorded their session is Mr. Bernie Marsden, Cozy Powell, Neil Murray, Don Airy, and believe it or not, doing backing vocals, David Coverdale.
Putting together a session for the Friday Rock Show, which we should be putting out, I think it's either in three or four weeks' time.
Down there in Studio 4 in Maida Vale, five of the greatest in the world of British rock and roll.
19 minutes after 11, let's read a few cards out here.
This is one from Kevin Rowland of 203 Queensway, Torquay, in Devon.
Say hello to Lemmy, Dennis, Angela the roadie, Glenn Fast Reaction Hawkins, Trace the simple Jordan, and Rachel Williams, who's got a birthday today.
Happy birthday, Rachel.
Al Whitaker lives in Barrow-in-Furness in Cumbria.
Wants to meet a bunch of a load of people up there in Barrow.
Particularly Wazza and Mac and Dicko and Shotta.
Or maybe that's Snotta, actually.
Disgusting.
Prattle and his old lady.

I'd say that one, Eric, and a special mention for his old lady by the name of Marie. Mind
you, I'd hate to have to say that again. That was Def Leppard from our archive set by them
from 1979, glad I'm alive. Before that, something from a new compilation album that's just out
on the market on the Decca record label.
The album has the title The Show of Our Lives.
The track you heard was called Hello, Hello.
The band are called Caravan.
Good compilation.
In a minute's time, it'll be 11.30,
which is the time that I should be reading out this letter.
Could I say happy birthday to Shelley, who was 15 on Monday?
Please also would I say hello to John and Chris, Lisa's friends,
and Jay and Julian, otherwise known as Jock,
and Ian and Richard and Greg and Sean and Trevor.
And will I thank Rainbow for a fantastic concert on the 27th of July at the Hammersmith Odeon
Lots of love to Janet and Lisa
Nice to see you at Windsor
This is a plea from the heart, it says here
It's a plea from the heart of David Serra
Of 14 Berryfield, Long Buckley and Northance
The plea goes like this
It is my 19th birthday on the 7th of August
I shall be listening to the Friday Rock Show as usual
Say hello to me
David, hello

And that's Demon from their first ever radio session
A very good session indeed
That track was called Decisions, who are Demon, I'll tell you.
Clive Cook on lead guitar, Malcolm Spooner on the rhythm guitar,
Paul Reilly on the bass, John Wright on the drums,
and Dave Hill doing the vocals.
Check their album out. It's on career.
But they were back in 1979.
Before that, something from an album called Something in the Night
by Pure Prairie League.
It's out on Casablanca. The track was called Don't Keep Me.
It's quarter to twelve.
A couple of girls I met outside Broadcasting House today
were really nice people. I thought they were anyway.
Good-looking, attractive, charming,
and their names are Susan Knight and Debbie Hartley.
It's nice to meet you, girls.
I'd also like to say hello to all the friends of,

who's up there in Tidworth and Hans.

Woodge the Closet Headbanger, whatever that is.
Bev's the We Don't Say That on this show
and Fraz and Bob and Jen.

and really is magnificent.
Listen to every single note as played by Jimi Hendrix.
Come on now, you know this is an interesting thing.

From the album Hendrix and the West,
to everybody who's into Hendrix,
Red House.

and so are quite a number of other people.
That's between 5 and 6.30.
Tony Wilson produces the Friday Rock Show.
He's not in the studio again tonight.
No, he's not getting lazy.
What happens now, on quite a number of Friday nights,
he actually has to be in the studio with a band tonight he's in there with bernie marsden and
